name: HTSP Tuner
description: Live TV and DVR for Jellyfin backed by Tvheadend over its native HTSP protocol. Unlike the official Tvheadend plugin -- which speaks HTSP only for metadata and then pulls the actual video over plain HTTP -- this plugin subscribes to the HTSP stream directly and remuxes the elementary streams into MPEG-TS inside Jellyfin. Codec, resolution, language and channel layout come straight from the HTSP subscriptionStart event; a short, one-time probe of the plugin's own muxed output then fills in the fields HTSP cannot carry -- interlacing, HDR/colour, bit depth, profile and level -- so Jellyfin makes correct direct-play, deinterlace and transcode decisions. All of this happens without opening a second tuner subscription. Supports fast same-multiplex channel switching, radio channels, live EPG pushed over async metadata, Tvheadend DVR timers and autorec series rules, channel tags, stream sharing between clients, and tuner/signal statistics.
overview: Native HTSP streaming from Tvheadend, remuxed locally to MPEG-TS.

versions.changelog:
### 1.0.0.0
- Initial release.
- Native HTSP subscription streaming with local MPEG-TS remuxing (no HTTP passthrough).
- Exact codec/resolution/language metadata from subscriptionStart; no ffprobe, no second
  tuner subscription.

- Fast same-multiplex channel switching and stream sharing.
- Radio channels, live EPG via async metadata, DVR timers, autorec series rules, channel
  tags and tuner/signal statistics.
